samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers to store intermediate values and control the multiplication process.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 4 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant name="size" value="4">Data width for inputs and outputs.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="partial_product_reg" width="8" description="Register to store partial products during multiplication." />
        <register id="sum_reg" width="8" description="Register to accumulate sums of partial products." />
        <register id="product_reg" width="8" description="Register to hold the final product result." />
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Extend input signals
        wire [7:0] a_extended = {4'b0000, mul_a};
        wire [7:0] b_extended = {4'b0000, mul_b};
        
        // Generate partial products
        genvar i;
        generate
            for (i = 0; i < size; i = i + 1) begin : pp_gen
                wire [7:0] partial_product;
                assign partial_product = (b_extended[i]) ? (a_extended << i) : 'd0;
                
                // Store partial products in register
                always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
                    if (!rst_n) begin
                        partial_product_reg[i] <= 0;
                    end else begin
                        partial_product_reg[i] <= partial_product;
                    end
                end
            end
        endgenerate
        
        // Add partial products
        integer j;
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                sum_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                sum_reg <= 0;
                for (j = 0; j < size; j = j + 1) begin
                    sum_reg <= sum_reg + partial_product_reg[j];
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Final product calculation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                product_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                product_reg <= sum_reg;
            end
        end
        
        // Assign output
        assign mul_out = product_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>
